name: Birnie Bus Svc Inc
principals: Tim Birnie (former President/CEO, joined Krapf board); founded by Martin K. Birnie and Martin J. Nunn
districts_or_customers: 70+ school districts and preschools across NY; 18 NY locations; also transit/paratransit/motorcoach contracts
recent_news: Acquired by Krapf Bus Companies (PA) effective June 14, 2017; became largest division of Krapf, which now operates ~2,500 buses across PA/NY/DE/VA.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. Already consolidated into Krapf Bus Cos (strategic acquirer). Could only be reached via a Krapf-level platform conversation.

name: Boston Student Transportation
owner_or_parent: Student Transportation of America (STA) / Student Transportation Inc., owned by CDPQ (Caisse de depot et placement du Quebec) and Ullico
principals: STA corporate leadership; no independent Boston principal identified
districts_or_customers: Greater Boston/Massachusetts student transportation operations under STA umbrella; STA also has MA presence in Acton
recent_news: Website ridestbus.com redirects to ridesta.com (Student Transportation of America). STA was taken private in Feb 2018 by a group led by CDPQ; remains CDPQ-owned. The Boston listing appears to be an STA branch/legal-entity address, not an independent operator.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. This is an STA (CDPQ-owned) local operating entity, not an independent family business. Listed Boston address is a corporate/registered office (84 State St) rather than a separate operating yard.

name: Midland Columbia Bus Co
owner_or_parent: Landmark Student Transportation (Ontario, Canada), majority-owned by CC&L Infrastructure (Connor, Clark & Lunn Infrastructure) since Jan 2022
principals: Founded by Bill & Peggie Flatt (1956); Flatt family (Doug, Bruce, Jeff Flatt) exited in 2015 sale to Landmark
districts_or_customers: Dallas SD2 (OR) plus districts across OR/WA/ID including Twin Falls and Filer ID; also operates Western States Bus Services subsidiary; historically ~23 districts across rural Pacific Northwest
recent_news: Flatt family sold MidCo to Landmark Student Transportation in 2015; CC&L Infrastructure acquired majority of Landmark in January 2022. In 2022, Pendleton SD ended its long-running contract with MidCo. Listed Dallas, OR address is one of multiple OR/WA/ID terminals; corporate HQ is Pendleton, OR.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. Already consolidated under Landmark / CC&L Infrastructure (PE-owned platform). Could only be relevant via a Landmark-level platform conversation, not as an independent acquisition.

name: Apple Business Co
owner_or_parent: First Student Inc. (owned by EQT Infrastructure)
principals: Apple Bus founded by the Hutton family (legacy); acquired entity now integrated into First Student
districts_or_customers: 80+ school district customers across 8 states (MO, KS, TX, IL, AL, LA, MI, AK); ~33 locations including Mound City, MO terminal
recent_news: First Student signed agreement to acquire Apple Bus Company in February 2022; deal closed by summer 2022. The 'Apple Business Co' record at 204 Mound St, Mound City MO is one of Apple Bus's local terminals, now part of First Student. Website 'Firststudentinc.Com' in the source data confirms integration.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. Acquired by First Student (EQT) in 2022. This Mound City MO record is a local Apple Bus terminal now under First Student ownership.

name: George's Transportation Co
principals: Leon A. George II (Owner); second-generation family business founded by Leon George Sr. and wife Esther in 1948
districts_or_customers: Palmerton Area School District, Lehighton Area School District, Carbon Career & Technical Institute (Carbon County, PA)
recent_news: Multi-generational family operator; Leon A. George II expanded to a second location (Palmerton) in 1995. 2013 PA state safety/operations award noted. Operates as 'George's Busing Inc.' / 'Leon A. George II School Buses, Inc.' across Lehighton and Palmerton facilities; no recent M&A or distress news identified.
fit_notes: Viable Summit tuck-in candidate. Independent 2nd-generation family operator (founded 1948), ~58 buses across 2 PA districts in Carbon County. Small but clean: long-tenured district contracts, stable footprint in eastern PA — geographically complementary to Summit's PA presence. Leon A. George II is the namesake owner and likely facing succession decisions over the next 5-10 years.
